title: Project Plan: Digital Ethics Essay on Piracy
text:
Arguments for piracy: - Wider (equal) access to content in restricted areas (games banned in Australia, other censored content) - Wider distribution of content
Arguments against piracy: - Creators lose income - Are not incentivised to create as much content




Arguments for piracy: - Means for obtaining original efforts (manga, Blood, Taylor Swift catalogue) - Saves money (until costs become negligible to income) - Content fragmented across multiple platforms - Access to censored content and free media (Russia and China) - Archiving and saving information so that it doesn’t get forgotten (Anna’s Archive)
Arguments against piracy: - Unfair to people who follow the law (paying streaming fees) - Unregulated and risky: viruses and scams - Piracy sites need income too - Promote gambling





Renting (subscription) vs. owning
Media platform dependency: the media you purchase is locked into the platform
If a platform shuts down, what happens to the media you “own”?
Stop Killing Games initiative: online games can’t be played when providers shut down servers

Wikipedia: Stop Killing Games







What aspects of a media piece can you copyright, and what makes it distinct enough to be copyrightable?

Reference video

Does the size of the creator affect the morality of pirating? Is it okay to pirate from commercially successful companies vs. indie studios? Where is the limit?







Quarto website (main deliverable)
3000-4000 words
Minimum 5 credible sources (good mix)
Declare any AI usage
5-minute presentation of the website
Contribution statement






Three perspectives, each person takes one
Draft via pull requests
Write in bullet points initially
Deadline: 5 June 2026
